sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: DOWN.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 27, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves block right by ~5 cols (VERIFIED step 2)', 'LEFT': 'Moves block left by ~5 cols; works from rows 25-29 corridor opening but blocked from rows 30-44 (VERIFIED steps 6-22)', 'UP': 'Moves block up by 5 rows (VERIFIED step 4)', 'DOWN': 'Moves block down by 5 rows (VERIFIED step 8)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'white_cross': 'Static reference marker at left of gray room (rows ~31-33, cols ~19-22)', 'orange_blue_block': 'Movable object: 4x2 orange on top + 5x3 blue below, moves in 5-cell steps', 'dark_gray_corridor': 'L-shaped passage inside gray room: vertical segment at cols 29-33, horizontal at rows 40-44 cols 14-33', 'blue_templates': 'Two blue L-shapes in black frames (top-center and bottom-left) - purpose unknown'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'Yellow bar + red squares - likely timer/progress'}
Current level:
- World model: Level 1. Block now at rows 25-29, cols ~24-28 inside vertical corridor after LEFT from cols 29-33. Corridor opening at top (rows 25-29, cols 29-33) is the only entry point - LEFT is blocked from all other vertical positions. Block moves in 5-cell increments.
- Goal model: Move block through corridor to reach white cross area (cols 19-22). Block is now inside corridor and can continue LEFT.
- Recent findings: ['LEFT works only from rows 25-29 (corridor top opening)', 'Block entered corridor at cols 29-33 after UP to rows 25-29 then LEFT', 'Block continues moving LEFT through corridor']
- Open questions: ['How many more LEFT moves to reach white cross?', 'Does block need to reach exact white cross position or just enter corridor?']
- Plan: Continue moving LEFT through corridor until block reaches white cross area or level completes
- Strategy log: ['Abandoned corridor navigation from bottom/middle - LEFT blocked from rows 30-44. Found entry only at top opening rows 25-29.']
- Failed probes: ['LEFT from cols 34-38 at rows 35-39: blocked (step 6)', 'LEFT from cols 34-38 at rows 40-44: blocked (step 11)', 'LEFT from cols 34-38 at rows 30-34: blocked (step 16)', 'RIGHT,LEFT,LEFT sequence: no extra movement (step 18)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 26 actions; a strong score needs about 22 or fewer. You are 1.2x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The block moved DOWN and now the white cross is gone! Looking at the image, the block is now at rows 30-34, cols ~19-23, and the white cross that was at rows ~31-33, cols ~19-22 is no longer visible. This means the block has overlapped/covered the white cross!
This is likely the goal - to position the block so it covers the white cross. The level might be complete now, or I might need to do something else.
Let me check if the level is complete by looking at the result. The result showed "level_completed": false, so the level is not complete yet. But the white cross is gone, which is significant.
Wait, let me look more carefully at the image. The block is now at rows 30-34, and the white cross is gone. But the level is not complete. What else could the goal be?
Maybe I need to move the block to a specific position where it matches one of the blue L-shapes in the black frames? Or maybe I need to move the block to the bottom of the corridor?
Let me check the exact position of the block and see if there's anything else I need to do.
Actually, looking at the image more carefully, the block is now at rows 30-34, cols ~19-23. The white cross is covered. But the level is not complete. Maybe I need to move the block further, like DOWN into the horizontal corridor, or LEFT/RIGHT to a different position.
Let me try LEFT to see if the block can move left from this position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check block position - white cross is covered!
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('O', 'b'):
        print(f"Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check if white cross still exists
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        print(f"White node {n['id']}: boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Try LEFT to see if block can move from this position
action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves block right by ~5 cols", "LEFT": "Moves block left by ~5 cols; works from rows 25-29", "UP": "Moves block up by 5 rows", "DOWN": "Moves block down by 5 rows"}, "object_taxonomy": {"white_cross": "Static reference marker at left of room", "orange_blue_block": "Movable object (4x2 orange + 5x3 blue)", "dark_gray_corridor": "L-shaped passage inside gray room", "yellow_bar": "Timer"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "Yellow bar + red squares - timer"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Level 1. Block at rows 30-34, cols ~19-23. White cross covered by block. Level not complete yet.", "goal_model": "Cover white cross, then possibly move block to another position", "recent_findings": ["DOWN from rows 25-29 moved block to rows 30-34, covering white cross"], "open_questions": ["What happens after covering white cross? Need to move block further?"], "current_plan": "Try LEFT to see if block can move from this position", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": []}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
